summary:
The cell-rich zone is densely populated with multiple types of supporting cells; however, based on specific mentions focusing heavily on their presence there, it can be concluded that \textbf{fibroblasts} dominate numerically due to their critical role as structural support for dental tissue integrity around enamel-dentin interfaces. Agent1 reasoned through a process involving an understanding of anatomical details from referenced materials indicating high concentrations of certain cellular components like fibroblasts which were explicitly highlighted as being present abundantly compared to other potential candidates listed or implied by context clues related directly back towards answering what constitutes "most" common amongst these structures described clinically significant roles vis-a-vis maintaining overall health/structure at those particular locations along teeth surfaces/interfaces between different layers/tissues comprising them physically speaking during development/maintenance phases post-eruption into oral cavity environment etc..
